title: Spear adopts Qubes security strengths (not full Xen/dom0)
motto: Qubes is the gold standard for compartmentalization. Spear takes the principles; Field1 + KILROY + NEXUS C2 are the field-native substrate.

honesty.not_shipping: Full Qubes OS / Xen / qrexec / GUI domain isolation day one
honesty.adopting: Security model, policy shape, and operator habits that made Qubes the secure OS reference

principles.qubes: VMs for different trust levels (work, banking, disposable)
principles.spear: Trust domains as first-class: field / operator / network / media / disposable session profiles

principles.qubes: dom0 minimal; no general browsing in admin domain
principles.spear: NEXUS C2 loopback-only; autoelevate allowlist (no polkit prompt surface); no casual root shell
Disposable Field session flag + Queen profile isolation + no auto-open untrusted docs

principles.qubes: Split GPG, split USB, network VM
principles.spear: Planned: split crypto (Hostess), USB gate (device whitelist), network via NEXUS gatekeeper
C2 capability gates on destructive APIs; operator token for kill/update/field-switch

principles.qubes: dom0 carefully maintained, minimal attack surface
principles.spear: KILROY Field OS identity + NEXUS security guard; Field1 whole-disk SPEARMBR not casual vfat

principles.qubes: NetVM / no net by default for many qubes
principles.spear: C2 public_internet=false; firstboot does not open browser; Queen only on operator action
Field Drive explicit pick with model/serial; device-whitelist doctrine in NEXUS

implemented_now: Field1 claimed FFAT as security-relevant storage (not silent mount as vfat)
has-field-drive recognizes SPEARMBR/Field1 (no false 'required' when Field1 present)
firstboot: welcome only — no automatic browser/Queen
NEXUS C2: loopback 127.0.0.1:9477, always-war capabilities, fail-closed destructive routes
autoelevate allowlist instead of polkit prompt spam
next: Disposable session profile (tmpfs home overlay, wipe on logout)
Trust-domain labels on apps (Queen=network, Field Terminal=operator, GIMP=media)
USB/device whitelist gate before hotplug use
Split crypto path via Hostess for keys
Optional full Qubes-class hypervisor track later (separate product branch)

references.source_disk_history: Field1 was Qubes whole disk (FIELD_QUBES) → claimed FFAT after backup to Hostess
